description: Trigger and behavior cases for sensitive disclosure: data minimization, pre-model redaction, output-path echo/bleed checks, log redaction, provider training-on-inputs posture, canary leak tests.

cases.prompt: Our support AI passes the entire customer record into the prompt and logs the full prompt for debugging. Design the redaction/data-minimization so we stop leaking PII to the model and the logs.

assertions: Applies data minimization first: replaces the whole-record dump with only the fields the task needs, tokenizing identifiers where possible.
Designs a redaction pipeline that strips secrets/PII before the model call across input, retrieved content, and tool outputs.
Flags the unredacted prompt logging as a disclosure and requires redaction at emission, composing observability-operator.
Adds canary and cross-user leak tests with expected no-leak outcomes.

cases.prompt: We're about to send customer health data to a model API. Someone mentioned the provider might train on inputs. Is that a disclosure problem?

assertions: Identifies training-on-inputs as disclosure regardless of transport security and treats sending regulated health data that way as a blocking finding.
Requires confirming the provider posture (retention, training, residency) and minimizing/redacting before any send.
Routes the go/no-go decision to ai-governance-risk-reviewer / human-approval-boundary.

cases.prompt: Make sure our vector search only returns documents the querying user's tenant is allowed to see.

assertions: Retrieval-time authorization of which documents a user can fetch is rag-security-architect; this skill handles minimization/redaction of sensitive data once it's in the pipeline, not retrieval permission.

cases.prompt: Log every full prompt and response including any PII so we have complete debugging data, and we'll just restrict who can read the logs.

assertions: Rejects logging unredacted sensitive content: an unredacted prompt/response log is a disclosure surface even with access controls.
Requires redaction at emission (metadata, not sensitive content) rather than relying solely on log access restrictions.
Notes cross-tenant/cross-user exposure risk if such logs are ever broadened or exported.
